marketplace.empty: No plugins available in the marketplace
marketplace.confirmUninstall: Remove {{name}}? The plugin will be deleted from disk.
marketplace.installDialogBody: This will download, verify, and install the plugin on disk. You can enable it afterwards on the Installed tab.

empty.noPluginsDesc: Place plugin directories in the backend/app/plugins/installed/ folder
empty.selectPlugin: Select a plugin to view details

modal.enableDesc: This plugin requires the following permissions:

dashboardPanel.title: Dashboard Panel
dashboardPanel.description: Show this plugin's panel on the Dashboard. Only one plugin can use the panel at a time.

confirm.uninstall: Uninstall plugin "{{name}}"? This will remove its configuration.

docs.securityWarning: Heads Up — Plugins Run at Two Trust Levels
docs.securityWarningDescription: Bundled plugins ship with BaluHost and run in-process with full host access. External marketplace plugins are sandboxed, but you still grant them capability scopes — only install and grant what you trust.
docs.permissionDescFileWrite: Can create and modify files on your NAS
docs.permissionDescSystemExecute: Can run shell commands on the server
docs.permissionDescDbWrite: Can write to the BaluHost database
docs.permissionDescUserWrite: Can create, modify, or delete user accounts
docs.marketplaceHint: The Marketplace tab is the easier route: plugins are downloaded, checksum-verified, and installed automatically. BaluHost periodically checks whether updates are available for installed plugins.
docs.permissionsDescription: Bundled plugins declare which permissions they need. Dangerous permissions (marked in orange) give broad system access — only grant them to bundled plugins you trust.
docs.eventHooksDescription: Plugins can react to things happening on your NAS (file uploads, user logins, RAID alerts, etc.) by subscribing to event hooks. Requires the

permissionDescriptions.file:write: Write and modify files in storage
permissionDescriptions.device:control: Control smart home devices (on/off, brightness, etc.)

tiers.title: Trust tiers

bundled.label: Bundled (in-process, trusted)
bundled.description: Ship with BaluHost and run as Python inside the server with full host access (the permission model below). Maintained in-repo.

external.label: External (sandboxed)
external.description: Marketplace plugins run in an isolated subprocess as a low-privilege user with no network, reaching the host only through the capability scopes you grant — no host code, database, filesystem, or shell access.

tiers.scopesIntro: When you enable an external plugin, you grant a subset of these scopes in the enable dialog. Each scope is enforced by the sandbox.
tiers.scopesUnavailable: The scope catalog is currently unavailable.
tiers.installExternal: Install external plugins from the Marketplace tab (downloaded and checksum-verified). When you enable one, pick which of its requested capability scopes to grant.
tiers.installBundled: Bundled plugins ship with BaluHost; enabling one grants its declared permissions via the permission dialog.

picker.title: Grant capability scopes — {{name}}
picker.desc: This external plugin runs sandboxed. Choose which capability scopes to grant. Requested scopes are pre-selected; you can grant fewer.
picker.noScopes: This plugin requests no capability scopes.

read:system-info.label: System Info
read:system-info.description: Read system metrics and hardware information

read:storage.label: Storage Info
read:storage.description: Read storage usage and disk information

storage.label: Plugin Storage
storage.description: Read and write the plugin's own key-value storage

core.system_metrics.label: System Metrics
core.system_metrics.description: Query core system metrics through the capability bridge
